contributions.title: Separate declared domain capabilities from observed lifecycle state
capabilities: model attached and detached state independently from spawnable and detachable capabilities
keep stable domain identity separate from display labels
require explicit state observation before state-sensitive orchestration
contributions.rationale: Continuum already names execution domains but previously had no provider-neutral attached or detached state contract, so capability declarations could be mistaken for observed availability.

contributions.title: Evaluate attach detach and spawn as explicit domain actions
capabilities: gate attach detach and spawn against declared capabilities
treat repeated attach or detach requests as explicit idempotent decisions
emit a decision without applying the action
contributions.rationale: A non-mutating decision layer lets Continuum coordinate future adapters without becoming an unrestricted terminal or process controller.

contributions.title: Separate client startup selection from domain service auto-start
capabilities: avoid interpreting service auto-start as client attachment
require an explicit attach decision before spawning into a detached domain
keep operator startup preference outside observed domain state
contributions.rationale: WezTerm recommends explicit startup connection selection rather than treating automatic connection as an intrinsic domain property; Continuum now preserves the same boundary.

contributions.title: Reconcile remote and local resource identities with stale-map expiry
capabilities: map remote resource identifiers to local handles
expire stale mappings when local resources disappear
avoid treating remote and local identifiers as interchangeable
contributions.rationale: This is actionable for future multi-client Continuum work, but no remote workspace resource model exists yet, so implementation is intentionally deferred.
